---
title: "Google Trends"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-06"
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Google Trends demand scoring for WFDF taxonomy activities
# ============================================================

# Packages
pkgs <- c("gtrendsR", "dplyr", "purrr", "tibble", "stringr", "readr")
to_install <- pkgs[!sapply(pkgs, requireNamespace, quietly = TRUE)]
if (length(to_install) > 0) install.packages(to_install)

library(gtrendsR)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(tibble)
library(stringr)
library(readr)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) Settings
# ------------------------------------------------------------

time_window <- "2021-03-07 2026-03-06"   # explicit date range; more stable than "today 5-y"
geo_code    <- ""                        # worldwide
gprop_type  <- "web"
n_rep       <- 2                         # repeat pulls to reduce sampling noise
sleep_sec   <- 1
low_value   <- 0.5                       # treat "<1" as 0.5

# Score breaks on relative scale after anchoring to disc golf = 1
# Tuned for highly skewed niche-sport demand
score_google_demand <- function(x) {
  case_when(
    is.na(x)        ~ NA_integer_,
    x >= 0.50       ~ 5L,
    x >= 0.10       ~ 4L,
    x >= 0.02       ~ 3L,
    x >= 0.002      ~ 2L,
    x >= 0.0002     ~ 1L,
    TRUE            ~ 0L
  )
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) Activity dictionary
#    "activity" = taxonomy label
#    "term"     = Google Trends search phrase
# ------------------------------------------------------------

activity_dict <- tribble(
  ~activity,                                   ~term,
  "Ultimate",                                  "ultimate frisbee",
  "Beach Ultimate",                            "beach ultimate",
  "Goaltimate",                                "goaltimate",
  "Hot Box",                                   "hot box frisbee",
  "World War",                                 "world war frisbee",
  "Double Disc Court (DDC - Classic or Escape)","double disc court",
  "Guts",                                      "guts frisbee",
  "Flutter Guts",                              "flutter guts frisbee",
  "DodgeDisc",                                 "dodgedisc",
  "Battle Royale",                             "battle royale frisbee",
  "Discflect / KanJam",                        "kanjam",
  "Frisbee Hoops",                             "frisbee hoops",
  "Polish Horseshoes / BeersBee",              "beersbee",
  "Frisbee H-O-R-S-E",                         "frisbee horse",
  "Accuracy",                                  "accuracy frisbee",
  "Disc Golf",                                 "disc golf",
  "Speed Lid Golf",                            "speed lid golf",
  "Distance",                                  "distance frisbee",
  "Self-Caught Flight (MTA, TRC)",             "self caught flight frisbee",
  "Discathon",                                 "discathon",
  "Freestyle",                                 "freestyle frisbee",
  "Canine Disc (Disc Dog)",                    "disc dog",
  "Overall",                                   "overall flying disc"
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3) Anchor groups
#    Each group shares at least one bridge term with another group.
#    Disc golf is the global anchor = 1.
# ------------------------------------------------------------

groups <- list(
  g1 = c("disc golf", "ultimate frisbee", "kanjam", "freestyle frisbee"),
  g2 = c("ultimate frisbee", "beach ultimate", "goaltimate", "hot box frisbee"),
  g3 = c("ultimate frisbee", "guts frisbee", "double disc court", "dodgedisc"),
  g4 = c("guts frisbee", "flutter guts frisbee", "battle royale frisbee", "beersbee"),
  g5 = c("kanjam", "frisbee horse", "frisbee hoops", "accuracy frisbee"),
  g6 = c("disc golf", "speed lid golf", "distance frisbee", "discathon"),
  g7 = c("freestyle frisbee", "disc dog", "self caught flight frisbee", "overall flying disc"),
  g8 = c("hot box frisbee", "world war frisbee", "goaltimate", "battle royale frisbee")
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 4) Helper to clean Google Trends hits
# ------------------------------------------------------------

clean_hits <- function(x, low_value = 0.5) {
  case_when(
    x == "<1" ~ low_value,
    x == ""   ~ 0,
    TRUE      ~ suppressWarnings(as.numeric(x))
  )
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 5) Pull and summarize one group
# ------------------------------------------------------------

gt_pull_summary <- function(keywords,
                            time,
                            geo = "",
                            gprop = "web",
                            n_rep = 2,
                            low_value = 0.5,
                            sleep_seconds = 1,
                            verbose = TRUE) {

  one_pull <- function(rep_id) {
    if (verbose) {
      message("Pulling: ", paste(keywords, collapse = " | "), " [rep ", rep_id, "]")
    }

    out <- tryCatch(
      gtrends(keyword = keywords, time = time, geo = geo, gprop = gprop),
      error = function(e) {
        message("  Failed: ", paste(keywords, collapse = " | "),
                " [rep ", rep_id, "] -> ", conditionMessage(e))
        NULL
      }
    )

    if (is.null(out) || is.null(out$interest_over_time) || nrow(out$interest_over_time) == 0) {
      return(NULL)
    }

    as_tibble(out$interest_over_time) %>%
      transmute(
        date = as.Date(date),
        keyword = as.character(keyword),
        hits_num = clean_hits(hits, low_value = low_value),
        rep = rep_id
      ) %>%
      filter(!is.na(hits_num))
  }

  pulls <- map(seq_len(n_rep), function(i) {
    if (i > 1) Sys.sleep(sleep_seconds)
    one_pull(i)
  }) %>% compact()

  if (length(pulls) == 0) return(NULL)

  dat <- bind_rows(pulls)

  # Summaries by keyword within each replicate
  rep_summaries <- dat %>%
    group_by(keyword, rep) %>%
    summarise(
      mean_hits = mean(hits_num, na.rm = TRUE),
      pct_zero  = mean(hits_num == 0, na.rm = TRUE),
      pct_lt1   = mean(hits_num == low_value, na.rm = TRUE),
      max_hits  = max(hits_num, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    )

  # Average over replicates
  rep_summaries %>%
    group_by(keyword) %>%
    summarise(
      mean_hits = mean(mean_hits, na.rm = TRUE),
      sd_hits   = sd(mean_hits, na.rm = TRUE),
      pct_zero  = mean(pct_zero, na.rm = TRUE),
      pct_lt1   = mean(pct_lt1, na.rm = TRUE),
      max_hits  = mean(max_hits, na.rm = TRUE),
      n_nonzero_rep = sum(mean_hits > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
      n_rep = n(),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(query = paste(keywords, collapse = " | "))
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 6) Build anchored Google demand index
# ------------------------------------------------------------

build_gtrends_index <- function(groups,
                                base_term,
                                time,
                                geo = "",
                                gprop = "web",
                                n_rep = 2,
                                low_value = 0.5,
                                sleep_seconds = 1,
                                verbose = TRUE) {

  group_ids <- names(groups)
  if (is.null(group_ids)) group_ids <- as.character(seq_along(groups))

  summaries <- map2_dfr(groups, group_ids, function(kw, gid) {
    s <- gt_pull_summary(
      keywords = kw,
      time = time,
      geo = geo,
      gprop = gprop,
      n_rep = n_rep,
      low_value = low_value,
      sleep_seconds = sleep_seconds,
      verbose = verbose
    )
    if (is.null(s)) return(NULL)
    mutate(s, group_id = gid)
  })

  if (nrow(summaries) == 0) stop("No usable Google Trends data returned.")

  membership <- map2_dfr(groups, group_ids, ~ tibble(group_id = .y, keyword = .x))

  base_rows <- summaries %>% filter(keyword == base_term)
  if (nrow(base_rows) == 0) stop("Base term not found in returned summaries.")

  start_group <- base_rows %>%
    slice_max(mean_hits, n = 1) %>%
    pull(group_id)

  overlaps <- inner_join(
    membership %>% rename(group_from = group_id),
    membership %>% rename(group_to = group_id),
    by = "keyword",
    relationship = "many-to-many"
  ) %>%
    filter(group_from != group_to) %>%
    distinct(group_from, group_to, keyword)

  group_scale <- setNames(rep(NA_real_, length(group_ids)), group_ids)
  group_steps <- setNames(rep(Inf, length(group_ids)), group_ids)
  group_scale[start_group] <- 1
  group_steps[start_group] <- 0

  changed <- TRUE
  while (changed) {
    changed <- FALSE

    for (i in seq_len(nrow(overlaps))) {
      gf <- overlaps$group_from[i]
      gt <- overlaps$group_to[i]
      anchor <- overlaps$keyword[i]

      if (!is.na(group_scale[gf]) && is.na(group_scale[gt])) {
        from_val <- summaries %>%
          filter(group_id == gf, keyword == anchor) %>%
          pull(mean_hits)

        to_val <- summaries %>%
          filter(group_id == gt, keyword == anchor) %>%
          pull(mean_hits)

        if (length(from_val) == 1 && length(to_val) == 1 &&
            !is.na(to_val) && to_val > 0) {
          group_scale[gt] <- group_scale[gf] * (from_val / to_val)
          group_steps[gt] <- group_steps[gf] + 1
          changed <- TRUE
        }
      }
    }
  }

  out <- summaries %>%
    mutate(
      group_scale = unname(group_scale[group_id]),
      bridge_steps = unname(group_steps[group_id]),
      common_value = mean_hits * group_scale
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(common_value))

  out_best <- out %>%
    group_by(keyword) %>%
    arrange(bridge_steps, desc(mean_hits), .by_group = TRUE) %>%
    slice(1) %>%
    ungroup()

  base_value <- out_best %>%
    filter(keyword == base_term) %>%
    pull(common_value)

  membership %>%
    distinct(keyword) %>%
    left_join(out_best, by = "keyword") %>%
    mutate(
      GT_relative = common_value / base_value,

      # auxiliary index for extreme zero inflation
      GT_visibility = case_when(
        is.na(mean_hits) ~ NA_real_,
        TRUE ~ log10(mean_hits + 0.001) + 0.5 * (1 - pct_zero)
      ),

      GT_score = score_google_demand(GT_relative),

      GT_confidence = case_when(
        is.na(GT_relative) ~ "Insufficient signal",
        bridge_steps == 0 & n_nonzero_rep >= 2 & pct_zero < 0.50 ~ "High",
        bridge_steps <= 1 & n_nonzero_rep >= 1 ~ "Medium",
        TRUE ~ "Low"
      )
    ) %>%
    arrange(desc(GT_relative))
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 7) Run the model
# ------------------------------------------------------------

gt_results <- build_gtrends_index(
  groups = groups,
  base_term = "disc golf",
  time = time_window,
  geo = geo_code,
  gprop = gprop_type,
  n_rep = n_rep,
  low_value = low_value,
  sleep_seconds = sleep_sec,
  verbose = TRUE
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 8) Attach taxonomy activity names
# ------------------------------------------------------------

final_table <- activity_dict %>%
  left_join(gt_results, by = c("term" = "keyword")) %>%
  select(
    activity, term,
    GT_relative, GT_visibility, GT_score, GT_confidence,
    mean_hits, pct_zero, pct_lt1, max_hits,
    bridge_steps, group_id, query
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(GT_relative), activity)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 9) Optional manual review columns
# ------------------------------------------------------------

final_table <- final_table %>%
  mutate(
    GT_note = case_when(
      is.na(GT_relative) ~ "No reliable Google Trends signal with current search phrase/grouping",
      pct_zero >= 0.95 ~ "Extremely intermittent search signal",
      pct_zero >= 0.75 ~ "Sparse search signal",
      TRUE ~ "Stable enough for relative comparison"
    )
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 10) Print and save
# ------------------------------------------------------------

print(final_table, n = nrow(final_table))

write_csv(final_table, "wfdf_google_demand_scores.csv")

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 11) Optional quick plot
# ------------------------------------------------------------


library(ggplot2)

final_table %>%
  mutate(GT_plot = ifelse(is.na(GT_relative), 1e-6, pmax(GT_relative, 1e-6))) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = GT_plot, y = reorder(activity, GT_plot))) +
  geom_point(size = 2.8) +
  scale_x_log10(breaks = c(0, .0001, .001, .01, .1, 1),
                labels = c(0, "0.0001", .001, .01, .1, 1)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Relative Google demand (Disc Golf = 1, log scale)",
    y = NULL,
    title = "Google Trends demand for WFDF taxonomy activities"
  )

final_table %>%
  mutate(GT_plot = ifelse(is.na(GT_relative), 0, GT_relative)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = GT_plot, y = reorder(activity, GT_plot))) +
  geom_point(size = 2.8) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    trans = scales::pseudo_log_trans(base = 10),
    breaks = c(0, 1e-4, 1e-3, 1e-2, 1e-1, 1),
    labels = label_number()
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Relative Google demand (Disc Golf = 1, pseudo-log scale)",
    y = NULL,
    title = "Google Trends demand for WFDF taxonomy activities"
  )
```
